cmd/sink-worker.purpose: Binary entry point for the Kafka-to-ClickHouse sink pipeline: consumes raw ingest CloudEvents from Kafka via confluent-kafka-go, deduplicates them, batch-inserts into ClickHouse via streaming.Connector, and publishes EventBatchedIngest flush notifications to Watermill so the balance-worker can recalculate entitlements. Contains only wiring and startup — all sink logic lives in openmeter/sink.

patterns.name: Wire-generated DI via initializeApplication
patterns.description: wire.go declares Application struct and wire.Build call with //go:build wireinject tag; wire_gen.go is generated and must never be hand-edited. Add new dependencies by adding a field to Application and a provider set reference to wire.Build.

patterns.name: oklog/run.Group for actor lifecycle with reverse shutdown
patterns.description: main.go adds exactly three actors: app.Sink.Run/Close, app.TelemetryServer.ListenAndServe/Shutdown, and run.SignalHandler. Always call group.Run(run.WithReverseShutdownOrder()) — not plain group.Run() — to ensure Sink shuts down before telemetry.
patterns.example:
group.Add(func() error { return app.Sink.Run(ctx) }, func(err error) { _ = app.Sink.Close() })
err = group.Run(run.WithReverseShutdownOrder())

patterns.name: Viper + pflag config loading with conf.Validate() before Wire init
patterns.description: Config is loaded via v.Unmarshal into config.Configuration then conf.Validate() is called before initializeApplication. Never skip validation or read config after Wire initialization.
patterns.example: v.Unmarshal(&conf); conf.Validate(); app, cleanup, err := initializeApplication(ctx, conf)

patterns.name: metadata() provides binary identity for telemetry
patterns.description: The local metadata() function calls common.NewMetadata(conf, version, "sink-worker") to tag OTel telemetry and logs. Every cmd binary must provide this function; wire.go references it directly in wire.Build.
patterns.example: func metadata(conf config.Configuration) common.Metadata { return common.NewMetadata(conf, version, "sink-worker") }

patterns.name: All providers sourced from app/common, never local
patterns.description: wire.Build references only common.* provider sets (common.Sink, common.Streaming, common.ClickHouse, common.WatermillNoPublisher, etc.) and the local metadata/NewLogger functions. No domain service constructors belong in wire.go.

patterns.name: WatermillNoPublisher instead of full Watermill router
patterns.description: Sink worker publishes ingest flush events via NewSinkWorkerPublisher (a dedicated Kafka publisher) — it does not need the full system-events Watermill router. Use common.WatermillNoPublisher to keep wiring minimal.
patterns.example: common.WatermillNoPublisher  // in wire.Build — not common.Watermill or common.EventBus

patterns.name: Unconditional cleanup defer even on init error
patterns.description: The cleanup func returned by initializeApplication must be deferred unconditionally, even when err != nil. The wire_gen.go cleanup chain includes partial-initialization teardown that must run to avoid resource leaks.

key_file_guides.role: DI specification: declares Application struct fields and wire.Build provider list. Only edited when adding/removing app-level dependencies.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Build tag //go:build wireinject must be present — omitting it causes duplicate symbol errors with wire_gen.go. Never add business logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by `go generate ./...` (Wire). Contains the actual initializeApplication with ordered cleanup12()...cleanup() chain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never hand-edit. Cleanup functions are chained in reverse-construction order — manual edits break the chain silently.

key_file_guides.role: Entry point: config loading, Wire init, run group assembly, signal handling. Contains no domain logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: app.SetGlobals() must be called before any logging to initialize OTel globals. run.SignalHandler must be the last actor added.

key_file_guides.role: Provides version/revision/revisionDate globals populated by ldflags and debug.BuildInfo. Pattern shared across all cmd binaries.
key_file_guides.watch_for: //nolint:gochecknoglobals and //nolint:gochecknoinits suppressions are intentional — do not remove them.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic or domain service calls directly in main.go or wire.go — all logic belongs in openmeter/sink or app/common
Hand-editing wire_gen.go — fully generated; any manual change is overwritten by `go generate`
Introducing a goroutine outside run.Group — bypasses graceful shutdown and signal handling
Constructing domain services (e.g. sink.Sink, streaming.Connector) directly in wire.go instead of delegating to common.* provider sets
Using context.Background() inside the run group actors — the root ctx from main() must be passed through

decisions.decision: WatermillNoPublisher is wired instead of a full Watermill router
decisions.rationale: Sink worker only publishes ingest flush events via a dedicated Kafka publisher (NewSinkWorkerPublisher); it does not consume system events and does not need the full router or DLQ/retry middleware stack.

GlobalInitializer.SetGlobals() initializes OTel global meter/tracer/propagator in one call, ensuring all downstream packages that rely on otel globals are consistently configured before the run group starts.

decisions.decision: Only two long-running actors: Sink and TelemetryServer
decisions.rationale: Sink worker has a single responsibility (Kafka → ClickHouse). Telemetry server is a mandatory sidecar for health/metrics. Keeping the run group minimal reduces shutdown ordering complexity.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new background actor (e.g. a reconciler) to the sink worker
